channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Harris urges Black churchgoers in Georgia to head out to vote and gets an assist from Stevie Wonder
item.description: JONESBORO, Ga. (AP) — Kamala Harris on Sunday summoned Black churchgoers to turn out at the polls and got a big assist from music legend Stevie Wonder, who rallied congregants with a rendition of Bob Marley’s “Redemption Song.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump works the fry station and holds a drive-thru news conference at a Pennsylvania McDonald's
item.description: FEASTERVILLE-TREVOSE, Pa. (AP) — Republican presidential nominee Donald Trump manned the fry station at a McDonald's in Pennsylvania on Sunday before staging an impromptu news conference, answering questions through the drive-thru window.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and MARC LEVY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris invokes Jimmy Carter in bid to get supporters to vote early
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris told supporters in Atlanta, Georgia, Saturday that former President Jimmy Carter had already cast his ballot in the election and they can too.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Voting groups that got a boost from Harris' candidacy are still working to persuade voters of color
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Left-leaning voter engagement groups that saw a surge in support and energy after Vice President Kamala Harris replaced President Joe Biden as the Democrats' White House nominee are aligning with a key pillar of her campaign in the final stretch before Election Day — trying to turn
item.dc:creator: BY AYANNA ALEXANDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Balance of power: Helene could shift political winds toward Trump, North Carolina lawmakers say
item.description: Two North Carolina officials are hopeful their state will see massive voter turnout despite the effects of Helene, and both suggested they think Donald Trump will win.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Oregon elections office shuts down phone lines amid false info surrounding Trump, voters' pamphlet
item.description: The Oregon Elections Division is shutting down its phone lines after the system was "overwhelmed" by out-of-state callers responding to false information about why Donald Trump is not in the Oregon Voters' Pamphlet.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Kustura, NewsNation

item.title: Trump compares imprisoned Jan. 6 rioters to Japanese Americans during WWII
item.description: Nearly 900 people have pleaded guilty to a variety of federal charges in relation to the events of Jan. 6.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats look to quash threat from Green Party's Jill Stein
item.description: Democrats are sounding the alarm over Green Party candidate Jill Stein as they look to avoid a repeat of the 2016 presidential election, in which Stein was accused of playing spoiler in key swing states. For months, Democrats had trained their ire on Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, NewsNation

item.title: Democratic Party eyes Swifties with new youth voter campaign
item.description: The DNC is hoping to tap into the star's pool of young people and get them excited about the election.
item.dc:creator: Nick Smith, NewsNation

item.title: North Carolina sees record turnout for early voting after hurricane
item.description: "The conditions improve each and every day," the executive director of North Carolina's State Board of Elections said.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kooiman, NewsNation

item.title: Swing state official warns village struggling with financial losses after influx of illegal immigrants
item.description: The village of Lockland, Ohio says it is struggling with an influx of migrants from Mauritania, with what it says are dire financial consequences for the area.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hovde shines spotlight on Tammy Baldwin's Wall Street partner during Wisconsin debate
item.description: Eric Hovde hammered Sen. Baldwin for not disclosing her longtime partner's finances, pointing out her position as a Wall Street wealth adviser.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Latest: Trump and Harris are campaigning for votes in pivotal Michigan
item.description: With the Nov. 5 election fast approaching, Democrat Kamala Harris and Republican Donald Trump are setting their sights on the key battleground state of Michigan on Friday. The vice president began her day in Grand Rapids before holding events in Lansing and Oakland County, northwest of Detroit.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Exclusive: Chauncey Parker talks new role as NYC deputy mayor for public safety
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams on Tuesday appointed Chauncey Parker to be New York City’s new deputy mayor for public safety. Parker takes over the position that was held by Philip Banks, who resigned the week prior.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Kentucky Tiktok suit says company considered using KY creators to sway Sen. Mitch McConnell
item.description: The suit says videos by Kentucky small businesses were identified as possibly of interest to the senator, who has expressed concern about the app's effect on kids
item.dc:creator: Sylvia Goodman, MPR News

item.title: Pro-Trump yard sign leads to shooting in Las Vegas neighborhood
item.description: Two men were playing about the former president titled "[Expletive] Donald Trump" when they decided to park their car outside the pro-Trump house.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, NewsNation

item.title: Politics Friday: The 2024 election will determine who presides in Minnesota courtrooms, remember there are judges on your ballot
item.description: There are about 100 races on Minnesota’s election ballots that won’t get that much attention, and they’re all about judgeships. MPR News politics editor and his guests talk about judicial elections and why they’re different here than in other states.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst, Matthew Alvarez, and Ellie Roth, MPR News

item.title: Submit your questions for the JD Vance town hall on NewsNation
item.description: X users can use the hashtag #VanceTownHall to submit questions to JD Vance, moderated by Chris Cuomo.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: 5 takeaways from the Nevada Senate debate
item.description: Sen. Jacky Rosen (D-Nev.) and Republican Sam Brown duked it out during their first and only debate Thursday night, making their pitches to voters in one of the key battleground states on the 2024 map and a must-win state for Democratic Senate majority hopes.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Trump family will get 75% of crypto token revenue
item.description: The Trump family's cryptocurrency platform, World Liberty Financial, indicated in a report released Thursday that DT Marks DEFI LLC, a Delaware-based company connected to the former president, could get 75 percent of WLF's protocol revenues.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Barbed wire, high fences: some election spots in Arizona close, but others step up
item.description: Some election locations in Arizona are shutting their doors in the face of security threats — but others are expanding their footprint.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: How to avoid sharing election misinformation
item.description: The 2024 election season is here. As we’re waiting to learn the final results, it can be easy to be duped by false or misleading information. Here are four tips to help you avoid spreading election related misinformation.
item.dc:creator: Audrey Nguyen, MPR News

item.title: Harris interview on Fox gets testy — but also gives her a do-over
item.description: The interview was the vice president's first formal sit-down with Fox. She faced questions on immigration, and was given a chance to answer again what she would do differently from President Biden.
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith, MPR News
